title: caltha palustris var. enkoso
intro: Caltha palustris var. enkoso, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It is used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, with its health benefits attributed to its high content of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as its flavonoids and saponins. Herbal preparations of Caltha palustris var. enkoso include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
